description: Bridge layer of OCCTSwift: converts kernel objects (Shape, Curve2D/Curve3D, Surface, Wire) and point clouds into ViewportBody values for the OCCTSwiftViewport Metal renderer, plus CADFileLoader.
rules: Import the module with `import OCCTSwiftTools`; add `import OCCTSwift` when you also touch kernel types (Shape, Curve2D/Curve3D, Surface, Wire) and `import simd` for SIMD vectors.
Every converter is a `public enum` used as a namespace of `static func`s — call them statically (e.g. `PointConverter.pointsToBody(...)`), there are no instances to construct.
The package's role is the OCCTSwift kernel -> ViewportBody bridge; it is the only repo depending on both OCCTSwift and OCCTSwiftViewport, sitting below OCCTSwiftAIS in the layered stack.

`PointConverter.pointsToBody(_:id:color:pointRadius:perPointColors:)` returns an Optional: nil when `perPointColors` is non-nil and its length != `points.count`. Empty input is valid and returns an empty point body for clearing a prior point set.

`CurveConverter`, `SurfaceConverter`, and `WireConverter` produce edge-only or isoparametric-grid bodies (no mesh). `curve2DToBody` projects onto the XZ ground plane (Y=0); `surfaceToGridBodies` returns up to two bodies `<idPrefix>-u`/`<idPrefix>-v`.

`CADFileLoader.shapeToBodyAndMetadata(_:id:color:...)` returns (ViewportBody?, CADBodyMetadata?); nil only if meshing fails AND no edge polylines extract. stl/deflection/gpuTessellation steer mesh density; edgeDeflection/maxPointsPerEdge tune edges.

`CADFileLoader.load(from:format:progress:)` (async throws) over OCCTSwiftIO ShapeLoader, returns CADLoadResult (bodies, metadata, shapes, PMI: dimensions/geomTolerances/datums). STL/IGES self-heal, STEP/OBJ/BREP not. Cancel throws ImportError.cancelled.

For headless, shape-only work with no Viewport dependency, skip CADFileLoader and use `OCCTSwiftIO.ShapeLoader.load` / `ShapeLoadResult` directly — CADFileLoader exists specifically to add the ViewportBody bridge on top.
